title: Update Cargo.toml to Rust 2024 edition
description: Change edition from 2021 to 2024 in Cargo.toml. This enables Rust 2024 edition defaults as required by STYLE_GUIDE.md.

title: Rename project: Update src/*.rs files from offload to offload
description:
Update all references in Rust source files from 'offload' to 'offload'. This includes:
- src/main.rs: CLI name, default config file, use statements
- src/lib.rs: crate docs and examples
- src/config.rs: doc comments
- src/config/schema.rs: struct names (OffloadConfig -> OffloadConfig), doc comments
- src/bundled.rs: cache directory paths
- src/report.rs: doc examples
- src/report/junit.rs: testsuite default name
- src/provider.rs, src/provider/*.rs: doc examples
- src/framework.rs, src/framework/*.rs: doc examples
- src/orchestrator.rs, src/orchestrator/*.rs: doc examples and sandbox ID prefix
- src/connector.rs: doc examples

The struct OffloadConfig should be renamed to OffloadConfig, and the field 'offload' in Config should be renamed to 'offload'.

title: Pipeline sandbox creation with early batch execution
description:
## Directive IMPORTANT: Before doing ANY git or VCS operations, you MUST activate the jujutsu skill by running /jujutsu. This is a jujutsu-managed repository. Using raw git commands will corrupt data. When this bead is complete, mark the final revision with a branch: danver/pipeline-sandbox-creation ## Problem The orchestrator creates all sandboxes upfront before beginning any test execution. With 200 sandboxes, this takes 8.3 seconds. During this time, zero tests are running. Since the LPT scheduler sorts batches heaviest-first, the longest batches (55-67s acceptance tests) are delayed by the full sandbox creation time. ## Evidence from Trace Analysis From Run 3 (200 sandboxes, 525 batches): - Sandbox pool creation: 8.34s - This is now the LARGEST remaining setup phase (after overlapped discovery was implemented) - The first batch does not start until 14.9s into the run - The longest batch (67.6s) starts at ~15s and finishes at ~83s - If it had started at ~7s instead, it would finish at ~75s, saving ~8s off the critical path The sandbox creation happens in `src/orchestrator/pool.rs` lines 40-60: ```rust pub async fn populate

( &mut self, count: usize, provider: &P, config: &SandboxConfig, ) -> Result<(), ProviderError> where P: SandboxProvider, { let futures: Vec<_> = (0..count) .map(|i| { ... provider.create_sandbox(&cfg).await }) .collect(); let sandboxes = futures::future::try_join_all(futures).await?; self.sandboxes.extend(sandboxes); Ok(()) } ``` And in `src/orchestrator.rs`, `run_with_tests` takes an already-populated `SandboxPool` (line 165). The pool is fully populated before `run_with_tests` is called (in `src/main.rs`). ## Required Changes ### 1. Investigate the current flow in main.rs Read `src/main.rs` to understand where `SandboxPool::populate` is called relative to `orchestrator.run_with_tests`. Map the full sequence: 1. Where is the pool created? 2. Where is it populated? 3. Where is it passed to the orchestrator? 4. What happens between populate and run_with_tests? ### 2. Design the pipelining approach The goal is to start executing batches on early-ready sandboxes while remaining sandboxes are still being created. Two approaches: **Option A: Streaming sandbox creation into the queue** - Modify `run_with_tests` to accept sandboxes via a channel instead of a pre-populated pool - Launch sandbox creation in the background, sending each sandbox to the channel as it becomes ready - Workers pull from the channel as sandboxes arrive **Option B: Two-phase pool population** - Create a small initial pool (e.g., 10-20 sandboxes) synchronously - Start execution with the initial pool - Continue creating remaining sandboxes in the background, adding them to the pool as they become ready - Workers check for new sandboxes periodically **Option C: Overlap pool creation with scheduling** - Run `populate` concurrently with duration loading + scheduling (which takes ~0.3s) - This is a minimal change but only saves ~0.3s Choose whichever option best fits the existing architecture. Option A is most elegant but requires the most refactoring. Option B is a good middle ground. Option C is low-effort but low-impact. ### 3. Implement the chosen approach Implement with proper error handling. If a sandbox fails to create mid-execution, existing running sandboxes should continue. The orchestrator should work with fewer sandboxes than requested (just slower). ### 4. Add tests - Test that execution starts before all sandboxes are ready - Test that the system works correctly with fewer sandboxes than requested (partial creation failure) - Test that all batches complete even if sandbox creation is slow ### 5. Preserve trace events The current trace emits `sandbox_pool_create` as a single span. With pipelining, this span should either: - Cover the full creation time (from first to last sandbox) - Or be replaced with per-sandbox creation events ## Expected Impact - Saves 5-8s off the critical path by overlapping sandbox creation with early batch execution - The heaviest batches (acceptance tests at 55-67s) start sooner, reducing total wall clock - Improvement is proportional to sandbox count (more sandboxes = more savings) ## Files to Modify - src/main.rs (understand current flow, possibly restructure) - src/orchestrator.rs (modify run_with_tests to accept streaming sandboxes) - src/orchestrator/pool.rs (possibly add streaming creation method)

title: Investigate and instrument slow acceptance tests for optimization
description:
## Directive

IMPORTANT: Before doing ANY git or VCS operations, you MUST activate the jujutsu skill by running /jujutsu. This is a jujutsu-managed repository. Using raw git commands will corrupt data.

When this bead is complete, mark the final revision with a branch: danver/investigate-slow-acceptance-tests

## Problem

Five acceptance tests individually take 47-68 seconds each, forming the hard floor on execution time. No amount of parallelism or scheduling improvement can reduce the wall clock below the duration of the slowest single test. These tests are the dominant bottleneck in Run 3.

## Evidence from Trace Analysis

From Run 3 (26,210 tests, 200 sandboxes):

| Batch | Tests | Duration | Sandbox |
|-------|-------|----------|---------|
| batch_3 | 1 | 67.6s | 17 |
| batch_1 | 1 | 58.1s | 2 |
| batch_0 | 1 | 48.0s | 0 |
| batch_4 | 1 | 48.0s | 32 |
| batch_2 | 1 | 47.6s | 3 |

These 5 tests consume 269 sandbox-seconds. The longest (67.6s) is 72% of the entire 93.8s execution window. Even a 2x improvement on just the slowest test would save ~34s off the critical path.

## Context

These tests are in the `mng` repository (the repo that uses offload to run its tests), not in the offload repository itself. The offload tool runs whatever tests it discovers -- it does not control their content. However, we can instrument offload to help identify what makes these tests slow.

## Required Changes

### 1. Add per-test timing to batch output

Currently, offload knows the total batch duration but not individual test durations within a batch. For single-test batches this is fine, but for multi-test batches the per-test breakdown is invisible.

In `src/orchestrator/runner.rs`, after downloading the JUnit XML results, parse the `time` attribute from each `<testcase>` element and log the top-N slowest tests:

```rust
// After downloading junit.xml, log slowest tests
let mut test_times: Vec<(&str, f64)> = Vec::new();
// Parse <testcase name="..." time="..."> elements
// Sort by time descending
// Log top 5 slowest
for (name, time) in test_times.iter().take(5) {
    info!("[SLOW TEST] {}: {:.1}s", name, time);
}
```

### 2. Add a `--slow-test-threshold` CLI flag

Add a `--slow-test-threshold` flag (default: 30s) that causes offload to emit a warning for any test exceeding the threshold:

```
WARNING: Test 'test_full_acceptance_flow' took 67.6s (threshold: 30s)
```

This makes slow tests visible in CI output without requiring trace analysis.

### 3. Add slow test data to the Perfetto trace

In the trace output, add per-test duration events. Currently the trace has batch-level events (`exec_batch`, `download_results`). Add individual test events within the exec thread:

```rust
// For each testcase in the junit XML:
tracer.complete_event(
    test_name,
    "test",
    sandbox_pid,
    TID_EXEC,
    test_start_us,
    test_duration_us,
);
```

This requires parsing the JUnit XML for individual test times and mapping them back to the trace timeline. The start time can be approximated (batch_start + cumulative_previous_test_times).

### 4. Add a summary section to the run output

After the existing summary (passed/failed/flaky counts), add a "Slowest Tests" section:

```
Slowest tests:
  1. test_full_acceptance_flow  67.6s
  2. test_end_to_end_pipeline   58.1s
  3. test_modal_integration     48.0s
  ...
```

Use the JUnit XML `time` attributes as the source of truth.

### 5. Write tests

- Test that the slow test warning is emitted when a test exceeds the threshold
- Test that the slow test summary is correctly sorted and limited to top N
- Test that per-test trace events are emitted correctly

## Expected Impact

- No direct wall-clock improvement (this is instrumentation)
- Enables the mng team to identify and profile the specific slow tests
- The slow test warnings in CI output will create visibility and pressure to fix them
- Per-test trace events enable deeper analysis in Perfetto UI

## Files to Modify
- src/orchestrator/runner.rs (add per-test timing extraction from JUnit XML)
- src/main.rs (add --slow-test-threshold flag)
- src/report.rs or src/report/junit.rs (add slow test summary to output)
- src/trace.rs (possibly add per-test trace events)

title: Add vitest duplicate test name check to onboarding skill
description: Update SKILL.md to detect vitest framework and check for duplicate space-separated test IDs during onboarding. If duplicates are found, the agent must stop and ask the user if they want the agent to deduplicate them by renaming tests more verbosely. Convey that this is a blocking requirement for using Offload.

title: Add offload collect verification step to onboarding skill
description: Update SKILL.md Step 10 (Run Offload Locally and Verify) to instruct agents to use 'offload collect' first to verify discovery works before running full 'offload run'. The agent should iterate on offload collect until discovery succeeds before attempting execution.

title: Strip pytest framework config to bare minimum
description: Remove python, extra_args, markers fields from PytestFrameworkConfig. Make command required (not Option). Keep test_id_format as internal constant. Update all related code: schema.rs, pytest.rs, main.rs init template, example TOML configs, tests, README.

title: Add CostEstimate struct to provider.rs with cpu_seconds and estimated_cost_usd fields. Include Display impl. The struct should be Clone, Debug, Default.
Track sandbox creation time in DefaultSandbox by adding a created_at: Instant field, set in DefaultSandbox::new. Update DefaultProvider::create_sandbox to pass Instant::now().

Add cost_estimate() -> CostEstimate method to Sandbox trait. Implement in DefaultSandbox using elapsed time from created_at and Modal pricing ($0.00003942/core/sec). LocalSandbox returns CostEstimate::default().

Add estimated_cost: CostEstimate field to RunResult. Aggregate costs from sandboxes during cleanup in orchestrator.rs. Update print_summary to accept optional show_cost bool and display cost when true.

Add --show-estimated-cost flag to Commands::Run in main.rs. Help text: 'Show estimated sandbox cost after run. Note: This is calculated client-side using simple formulas and may not reflect actual billing, discounts, or pricing adjustments.'
Wire --show-estimated-cost through run_tests -> dispatch_framework -> run_all_tests -> orchestrator. Pass show_cost to print_summary. Only display cost line when flag is set and cost > 0.

Add cpu_cores field to DefaultProviderConfig and ModalProviderConfig with default 0.125. Pass cpu_cores to DefaultSandbox for cost calculation. Update cost_estimate() to multiply by cpu_cores. The cpu_cores should also be injectable into command templates via {cpu_cores} placeholder.
description: Add cpu_cores field to ModalProviderConfig (default 0.125) and DefaultProviderConfig (default 1.0). Plumb cpu_cores through ModalProvider to modal_sandbox.py create via --cpu flag. Pass cpu_cores to DefaultSandbox for cost calculation. Update cost_estimate() to multiply by cpu_cores. Inject {cpu_cores} into DefaultProvider command templates. Trim wordy doc comments to one-line summaries.

title: Create skills/offload/SKILL.md
description: Create the /offload skill SKILL.md with frontmatter, overview, invocation guide, decision guide, exit codes, debugging, CLI reference, and config groups reference (~150 lines)

title: Create install-skills.sh
description: Create standalone bash installer that works both curl|bash and local. Symlinks in-repo, downloads from GitHub raw URLs standalone. Installs both /offload and /offload-onboard skills.

title: Modify justfile to delegate to install-skills.sh
description: Replace hardcoded install-skill recipe (lines 39-55) with install-skills recipe that calls ./install-skills.sh, plus install-skill alias for backward compat.

title: Replace flat default_duration with per-group average in scheduler
description: Replace the hardcoded 1s default_duration in schedule_lpt with per-group average durations computed from historical data. Steps: (1) Add group() accessor to TestInstance. (2) Change schedule_lpt signature to accept HashMap<String, Duration> for group defaults instead of a single Duration. (3) In orchestrator.rs, compute per-group averages from the durations map + test records, and pass that mapping to schedule_lpt. (4) Update all scheduler tests. Keep the 1s fallback only when a group has zero historical data.

title: Rename project: Rename offload-*.toml config files to offload-*.toml
description:
Rename all configuration files with 'offload' prefix to use 'offload' prefix:
- offload.toml -> offload.toml
- offload-local.toml -> offload-local.toml
- offload-modal.toml -> offload-modal.toml

- offload-computronium-modal.toml -> offload-computronium-modal.toml
- offload-sculptor-modal.toml -> offload-sculptor-modal.toml

Also update the [offload] section in these files to [offload].

title: Rename project: Update README.md from offload to offload
description:
Update README.md to replace all references to 'offload' with 'offload'. This includes:
- Project title
- Feature descriptions
- Installation commands
- CLI examples (offload init, offload run, etc.)
- Configuration file references (offload.toml -> offload.toml)
- Example configuration sections ([offload] -> [offload])
- All documentation text

title: Rename project: Update scripts/modal_sandbox.py from offload to offload
description:
Update scripts/modal_sandbox.py to replace all references to 'offload' with 'offload'. This includes:
- Module docstring
- CLI help text
- Modal App names (offload-sandbox -> offload-sandbox, offload-rust-sandbox -> offload-rust-sandbox, etc.)
- Function docstrings
- Comments

title: Rename project: Update .gitignore and .beads/issues.jsonl from offload to offload
description:
Update supporting files:
- .gitignore: Change offload.local.toml to offload.local.toml
- .beads/issues.jsonl: Update any references to 'offload' in issue descriptions to 'offload'

title: Update hardcoded paths in config files to use @modal_sandbox.py syntax
description:
Update offload-sculptor-modal.toml and offload-computronium-modal.toml to replace hardcoded paths like:
  /Users/jacobkirmayer/imbue/shotgun-rs/scripts/modal_sandbox.py
with the portable syntax:
  @modal_sandbox.py

This removes developer-specific paths and uses the project's script lookup feature.

title: Add dockerfile_path field to DefaultProviderConfig
description:
Add an optional 'dockerfile_path' field (Option<String>) to DefaultProviderConfig. This field represents a relative path to a Dockerfile that can be used when creating Modal sandboxes from custom Docker images.

Include:
- The new field with serde attributes
- Doc comments explaining it's a relative path to a Dockerfile
- Default value of None

Files to modify:
- src/config/schema.rs (add field to DefaultProviderConfig)

title: Add --dockerfile option to modal_sandbox.py create commands
description:
Update modal_sandbox.py to support building images from Dockerfiles.

Add a --dockerfile option to the create commands (create default, create rust) that:
1. Takes a relative path to a Dockerfile  
2. Uses modal.Image.from_dockerfile() to build the image instead of the hardcoded image definition
3. Falls back to existing behavior when --dockerfile is not provided

The Dockerfile path should be resolved relative to the current working directory.

Files to modify:
- scripts/modal_sandbox.py (add --dockerfile option to create subcommands)

title: Update offload-modal.toml and offload-cargo-modal.toml to use Dockerfile
description:
Update the local example config files to use the new dockerfile_path feature.

Changes needed:
1. Add dockerfile_path = '.devcontainer/Dockerfile' to the [provider] section
2. Update create_command to pass the --dockerfile flag with the path

These are the 'local examples' - configs designed to work with the offload repo itself.

Files to modify:
- offload-modal.toml  
- offload-cargo-modal.toml

title: Expand dockerfile_path placeholder in create_command
description:
The create_command is run without expanding the {dockerfile_path} placeholder, causing a FileNotFoundError when trying to use the dockerfile_path feature.

In src/provider/default.rs, the create_sandbox method runs self.config.create_command directly without substituting {dockerfile_path}.

Fix: Before running the create_command, replace {dockerfile_path} with the actual value from self.config.dockerfile_path (or empty string if None).

Location: src/provider/default.rs, create_sandbox method around line 147

title: Rename config/mod.rs to config.rs (2018+ module style)
description: Rename src/config/mod.rs to src/config.rs following STYLE_GUIDE.md. The config directory contains schema.rs which should remain in src/config/. Update lib.rs module declaration accordingly.

title: Restructure modal_sandbox.py: separate dockerfile command from existing commands
description:
Currently --dockerfile is a flag on 'create default' and 'create rust'. This makes the build slow when dockerfile is used.

Restructure so that:
1. 'create dockerfile <path>' - new command that builds from a Dockerfile. Must validate the file exists and print sensible error if not.
2. 'create default', 'create rust', etc. - keep using their hardcoded modal.Image definitions (fast path)

This allows users to choose:
- Fast: use the built-in image definitions (coupled but fast)
- Flexible: use a Dockerfile (slow but decoupled)

Changes needed:
1. Remove --dockerfile option from create_default and create_rust
2. Add new 'create dockerfile' subcommand that takes a path argument
3. Update the TOML configs to use 'create dockerfile .devcontainer/Dockerfile' instead of 'create default --dockerfile {dockerfile_path}'

title: Bump ratchet no-bare-print budget for scripts
description:
The new create dockerfile command in modal_sandbox.py adds 2 print statements:
1. Error message to stderr when dockerfile not found
2. sandbox.object_id output (standard pattern for create commands)

These are legitimate uses in a CLI script. Bump the ratchet budget from 25 to 27.

File to modify: ratchet-counts.toml

title: Add image cache infrastructure to modal_sandbox.py
description:
Add infrastructure for caching Modal image IDs:

1. Add CACHE_DIR constant (~/.cache/offload/)
2. Add ImageCacheEntry dataclass with fields: image_id, dockerfile_hash, mtime, created_at
3. Add functions: get_cache_path(), load_cache(), save_cache(), compute_dockerfile_hash()
4. Cache is stored as JSON in ~/.cache/offload/modal-images.json

Files to modify: scripts/modal_sandbox.py only

title: Add build-image subcommand to modal_sandbox.py
description:
Add a new 'build-image' subcommand group that eagerly builds Modal images and caches their IDs:

1. Add @cli.group() for 'build-image' subcommand
2. Add build-image subcommands for each sandbox type (default, rust, dockerfile, computronium, sculptor, mngr)
3. Each subcommand:
   - Builds the image using image.build(app)
   - Extracts image.object_id
   - Computes dockerfile hash (if applicable)
   - Saves to cache via save_cache()
   - Prints the image_id to stdout

Files to modify: scripts/modal_sandbox.py only

Depends on: code-22

title: Modify create subcommands to use cached images
description:
Update each create subcommand to check for and use cached image IDs:

1. At the start of each create subcommand (default, rust, dockerfile, computronium, sculptor, mngr):
   - Load cache and check for valid cached image for this sandbox type
   - If cached and valid (hash matches, not expired), use modal.Image.from_id(cached_image_id)
   - If not cached or invalid, fall back to current behavior (build image inline)
   - Optionally update cache after successful sandbox creation

2. Add --force-rebuild flag to skip cache lookup

3. Print cache status to stderr (e.g., 'Using cached image im-xxx' or 'Building fresh image...')

Files to modify: scripts/modal_sandbox.py only

Depends on: code-22, code-23

title: Add cache-status and cache-clear subcommands
description:
Add utility subcommands for cache management:

1. Add 'cache-status' subcommand:
   - Shows all cached images with their IDs, sandbox types, creation dates
   - Shows cache file location
   - Indicates if images are valid/stale

2. Add 'cache-clear' subcommand:
   - Clears all cached image entries
   - Add optional --type flag to clear only specific sandbox type

Files to modify: scripts/modal_sandbox.py only

Depends on: code-22

title: Bump ratchet budget for no-dataclasses-import in scripts
description:
Bump the ratchet budget for no-dataclasses-import rule to allow dataclasses usage in scripts/modal_sandbox.py.

Add the following to ratchet-counts.toml:
[no-dataclasses-import]
"scripts" = 1

This is required for the image cache infrastructure (code-22) which uses @dataclass for ImageCacheEntry.

Files to modify: ratchet-counts.toml only

title: Change cache location from ~/.cache/offload to .offload/
description:
Update modal_sandbox.py to store the image cache in the current working directory instead of the user's home directory.

Changes required:
1. Change CACHE_DIR from Path.home() / '.cache' / 'offload' to Path.cwd() / '.offload'
2. Change CACHE_FILE from 'modal-images.json' to 'modal_images.json' (underscore for consistency)
3. Update any references to these constants

This makes the cache:
- Per-project instead of global
- Portable with the project
- Cross-platform compatible (no home directory issues)

Files to modify: scripts/modal_sandbox.py only

title: Add logging to distinguish image builds from sandbox creation
description:
Add tracing logs in the Rust code to provide visibility into when Modal images are being built vs when sandboxes are being created.

The create_command output goes through the DefaultProvider in src/provider/default.rs. We should:
1. Log when create_command is invoked
2. Parse stderr from the create_command to detect image building vs cached image usage
3. Log appropriately (e.g., 'Building image...' vs 'Using cached image' vs 'Creating sandbox...')

The modal_sandbox.py script prints status messages to stderr:
- 'Building image...' or 'Building image from Dockerfile...' when building
- 'Using cached image' when using cache
- 'Creating sandbox...' or 'Sandbox ready: <id>' when creating sandbox

Files to modify: src/provider/default.rs

title: Create ModalProvider struct in src/provider/modal.rs
description:
Create a new ModalProvider struct that handles: 1. Loading cached image IDs from .offload/modal_images.json using the cache module 2. Computing Dockerfile hash and checking cache validity 3. Delegating to Python for image building when cache miss 4. Storing new image IDs in cache 5. Creating sandboxes using cached image IDs. The provider should call modal_sandbox.py with appropriate commands for Modal operations only.

CRITICAL: Do NOT use unwrap() anywhere in production code. Use proper error handling with Result types and context. The ratchet budget for no-unwrap in src/provider is 2, and we cannot exceed it.

title: Rename discovery/mod.rs to discovery.rs (2018+ module style)
description: Rename src/discovery/mod.rs to src/discovery.rs following STYLE_GUIDE.md. The discovery directory contains cargo.rs, generic.rs, pytest.rs which should remain in src/discovery/. Update lib.rs module declaration accordingly.

title: Wire Modal provider into main.rs
description:
Update main.rs to instantiate ModalProvider when config type is 'modal'.

Requirements:
1. Import ModalProvider from provider::modal
2. Update the provider instantiation match arm to create ModalProvider for Modal config
3. Handle errors properly (ModalProvider::from_config returns ProviderResult)
4. Update the run_with_provider function to accept ModalProvider

Files to modify: src/main.rs

title: Update offload-modal.toml for new Modal provider type
description:
Update offload-modal.toml to use the new Modal provider type instead of default provider with Modal commands.

Change from:
[provider]
type = "default"
dockerfile_path = ".devcontainer/Dockerfile"
create_command = "uv run @modal_sandbox.py create dockerfile {dockerfile_path}"
exec_command = "uv run @modal_sandbox.py exec {sandbox_id} {command}"
destroy_command = "uv run @modal_sandbox.py destroy {sandbox_id}"

To:
[provider]
type = "modal"
app_name = "offload-sandbox"
image_type = { dockerfile = ".devcontainer/Dockerfile" }
timeout_secs = 600

Files to modify: offload-modal.toml

title: Clean up DefaultProviderConfig: remove dockerfile_path
description:
Remove dockerfile_path field from DefaultProviderConfig since it is now Modal-specific.

Requirements:
1. Remove dockerfile_path field from DefaultProviderConfig in src/config/schema.rs
2. Remove the placeholder replacement for {dockerfile_path} in src/provider/default.rs
3. Update any documentation that mentions dockerfile_path in default provider context

Files to modify:
- src/config/schema.rs
- src/provider/default.rs

title: Fix Modal provider image build synchronization
description:
When multiple sandboxes are created concurrently with a cache miss, all of them independently build the image instead of waiting for the first one to finish.

Fix by adding synchronization:
1. Add a build_in_progress mutex to track ongoing builds
2. First thread to see cache miss acquires the build lock and builds
3. Other threads wait on the build lock, then recheck the cache

This prevents redundant Modal API calls when max_parallel > 1.

title: Investigate Modal image ID caching quirk - image_id may not be final
description:
Modal has a known quirk where the image_id returned from build may not be the 'final' image ID. This causes cached images to still require some rebuild time. Investigate:
1. How Modal image IDs work (im-* format)
2. What happens when we call image.build(app) - is the returned ID interim?
3. Whether there's a way to get the truly final/hydrated image ID
4. What changes are needed to modal_sandbox.py and the Rust cache to store the correct ID

## Investigation Results

**Root Cause:** Modal images have two phases - lazy (local shell) and hydrated (fully resolved on server). When we get `object_id` after `.build()` without explicit `.hydrate()`, we may get an interim ID that isn't fully resolved on Modal's backend.

**Solution:** Add explicit `.hydrate()` calls:
1. After `image.build(app)`, call `image.hydrate()` before reading `object_id`
2. When reconstructing with `Image.from_id(cached_id)`, call `.hydrate()` before use

**Key findings:**
- `.build(app)` triggers build but may not fully resolve the image
- `.hydrate()` explicitly forces full hydration on Modal's servers
- `.from_id()` is lazy - returns local shell until hydrated
- Only after hydration is `object_id` guaranteed to be the final ID

title: Add .hydrate() calls to Modal image builds for proper caching
description:
Based on investigation in code-34, Modal images need explicit hydration to get the final image ID.

## Changes Required

### In scripts/modal_sandbox.py:

1. **All build functions** (build_dockerfile, build_preset, _build_default, _build_rust, _build_sculptor, etc.):
   After `image.build(app)`, add `image.hydrate()` before printing `object_id`:
   ```python
   image.build(app)
   image.hydrate()
   print(image.object_id)
   ```

2. **create_with_image function**:
   After `Image.from_id()`, hydrate before using in Sandbox:
   ```python
   image = modal.Image.from_id(image_id)
   image.hydrate()
   sandbox = modal.Sandbox.create(app=app, image=image, ...)
   ```

## IMPORTANT: Style Constraints
- **NO trailing comments** - the ratchet budget for `no-trailing-comments [scripts]` is 3
- Do NOT add comments on the same line as code
- If explanation is needed, add a comment on a SEPARATE LINE above the code
- Prefer no comments at all - `.hydrate()` is self-explanatory

## Why This Fixes the Issue
- `.build()` triggers the build but may return before full registration
- `.hydrate()` ensures the image is completely resolved on Modal's backend
- `.from_id()` is lazy - without hydration, it needs server resolution at use time
- With proper hydration, cached IDs will be truly 'final' and reuse will be instant

title: Fix Modal image hydration - cannot hydrate on demand
description:
The .hydrate() call fails with:
```
raise ExecutionError(
    "Images cannot currently be hydrated on demand; you can build an Image by running an App that uses it."
)
```

## Investigation Results
Modal images can only be hydrated by **using** them in an App (e.g., Sandbox.create()).
`.hydrate()` cannot be called on images directly.

## Solution Options
1. **Remove .hydrate() calls** - Accept slight overhead on reuse
2. **Create dummy sandbox** - Force hydration by briefly creating a sandbox:
   ```python
   image.build(app)
   temp_sandbox = modal.Sandbox.create(app=app, image=image, timeout=10)
   temp_sandbox.terminate()
   print(image.object_id)  # Now fully hydrated
   ```

## Chosen Approach
Per user suggestion, try Option 2: Create a minimal dummy sandbox to force hydration, then immediately terminate it. This ensures the cached image ID is truly final.

## Changes Required
Replace `.hydrate()` calls with dummy sandbox creation pattern in all build_* functions in scripts/modal_sandbox.py

title: Investigate 30-second delay between cache hit and image loading
description:
## Problem
There's a ~30 second pause between cache lookup and actual image loading:
```
19:54:14 INFO Using cached Modal image for dockerfile:.devcontainer/Dockerfile: im-YlDkL514ar5gcMsBUhUBTd
19:54:46 INFO Loading image im-YlDkL514ar5gcMsBUhUBTd...  <-- 32 seconds later!
```

## Investigation Results

### Code Flow
1. Rust: `get_or_build_image()` finds cached entry, logs 'Using cached Modal image', returns image_id immediately
2. Rust: `call_python_create_with_image()` spawns Python process
3. Python: `modal.App.lookup(app_name, create_if_missing=True)` - possibly slow?
4. Python: `modal.Image.from_id(image_id)` - THIS IS THE DELAY
5. Python: `modal.Sandbox.create()`

### Root Cause
The delay is in the Python script between receiving the image_id and creating the sandbox. Likely causes:
1. `modal.App.lookup()` may be doing network calls
2. `modal.Image.from_id()` needs to resolve/hydrate the image from Modal's servers
3. Python process startup overhead (uv, imports)

### Possible Solutions
1. **Add more logging** to pinpoint exactly which call is slow
2. **Pre-create the App** during image build, not sandbox creation
3. **Accept the latency** if it's inherent to Modal's API design
4. **Reuse Python process** instead of spawning new one each time (complex)

## Next Step
Add detailed timing logs to modal_sandbox.py to identify which specific call takes 30 seconds.

title: Add timing logs to create-with-image to identify 30s delay source
description:
Add detailed timing logs to `create_with_image()` in `scripts/modal_sandbox.py` to identify which call is slow:

```python
import time

@cli.command('create-with-image')
def create_with_image(image_id: str, sandbox_type: str):
    t0 = time.time()
    
    app_name = app_name_map.get(sandbox_type, 'offload-sandbox')
    print(f'[{time.time()-t0:.2f}s] Looking up app {app_name}...', file=sys.stderr)
    app = modal.App.lookup(app_name, create_if_missing=True)
    print(f'[{time.time()-t0:.2f}s] App lookup complete', file=sys.stderr)
    
    print(f'[{time.time()-t0:.2f}s] Loading image {image_id}...', file=sys.stderr)
    image = modal.Image.from_id(image_id)
    print(f'[{time.time()-t0:.2f}s] Image loaded', file=sys.stderr)
    
    print(f'[{time.time()-t0:.2f}s] Creating sandbox...', file=sys.stderr)
    sandbox = modal.Sandbox.create(app=app, image=image, ...)
    print(f'[{time.time()-t0:.2f}s] Sandbox created', file=sys.stderr)
```

This will show exactly which call takes the 30 seconds.

title: Bump scripts ratchet budgets for false positives (no-global-keyword, no-trailing-comments, no-if-elif-without-else)

- no-trailing-comments: Detects inline comments in dependency lists (6 violations)

title: Bump no-unwrap budget for src directory
description:
The src directory has 29 unwrap() violations but only 15 budget. Many of these are:
- In test code (cache.rs tests, bundled.rs tests)
- In Mutex lock patterns (report.rs, framework.rs) where lock poisoning is acceptable
- In Regex::new with static patterns that cannot fail

Bump the src budget from 15 to 29 to match current violations. Future work can refactor these.

title: Tighten ratchet budgets that exceed current violations
description: After fixing exceeded budgets, run 'ratchet tighten' to reduce budgets that have more allowance than actual violations. This ensures the ratchet only allows the current number of violations, not more.

title: Remove unwrap() calls from src/main.rs

These are Mutex::lock().unwrap() patterns. Replace with:
- .lock().expect() with descriptive message about lock poisoning, OR
- Custom error handling that propagates the error

Note: Mutex poisoning is rare but the style guide forbids unwrap().

title: Remove unwrap() calls from src/cache.rs tests

Remove 12 unwrap() calls from test functions in cache.rs.

Test functions at approximate lines: 217, 225, 239, 245, 333, 337, 338, 342, 348, 384, 391, 393, 397.

title: Remove unwrap() calls from src/provider/local.rs

- child.stderr.take().unwrap()

title: Remove unwrap() calls from src/framework/*.rs (Regex patterns)

src/framework/pytest.rs lines 205, 207-209, 219, 258 (6 calls)  
src/framework/cargo.rs lines 262, 290, 320 (3 calls)

These are static regex patterns that cannot fail at runtime. Options:
1. Use lazy_static! or once_cell to compile regexes once

title: Remove unwrap() calls from src/framework.rs (Mutex locks)

title: Update ratchet to version 0.2.4

title: Implement tar-based file transfer for Modal sandboxes
description:
Optimize Modal sandbox file uploads by using tar for file delivery.

## Background
Running tests via Modal is currently slow due to time spent uploading multiple files to the target. Each file is transferred individually via sandbox.open(), which is inefficient for large directory trees.

## Solution
1. On the client side: Create a tar archive of the directory to transfer
2. Transfer the single tar file to the sandbox
3. On the sandbox side: Extract the tar archive to the destination directory

## Implementation locations
- scripts/modal_sandbox.py: Update copy_dir_to_sandbox() function to use tar-based approach
- The tar module is already a dependency in Cargo.toml

## Expected improvements
- Reduced number of file operations (one tar vs many individual files)
- Faster transfer times due to fewer round trips
- Better handling of directory structures

## Files to modify
- scripts/modal_sandbox.py: copy_dir_to_sandbox() function

title: Add --copy-dir argument to run command for copying directories to Modal sandboxes
description:
Add a repeatable --copy-dir argument to the run command with the form --copy-dir='/from/dir:/to/dir'. This copies local directories to the Modal sandbox at sandbox creation time. Example: offload -c offload-modal.toml run --copy-dir='/tmp/patch:/offload_upload'. Requires changes to:
1. src/main.rs - Add --copy-dir argument to Run command
2. src/provider/modal.rs - Pass copy-dirs through to Python create command  
3. scripts/modal_sandbox.py - Add --copy-dir to create command and call copy_dir_to_sandbox

title: Change download() signature to accept multiple path pairs
description: Change Sandbox::download() from (remote: &Path, local: &Path) to accept a slice of path pairs. Update all implementations (DefaultSandbox, ModalSandbox, LocalSandbox) and the call site in try_download_results to use a single download call for all JUnit XML paths.

title: Create LICENSE file with all-rights-reserved for Imbue AI
Update Cargo.toml: metadata, include whitelist, license-file

title: Add env field to ModalProviderConfig and DefaultProviderConfig

title: Implement expand_env_value function for ${VAR} syntax
description:
Implement environment variable expansion in config loading. Add a function that expands ${VAR} and ${VAR:-default} syntax in string values.

Syntax to support:
- ${VAR} - required, fail if not set
- ${VAR:-default} - optional, use default if not set  
- $$ - escaped literal dollar sign

Files to modify:
- src/config/mod.rs or src/config/schema.rs (add expand_env_value function)
- Apply expansion when loading provider.env HashMap values

Acceptance criteria:
- Function handles all three syntax cases
- Clear error message when required var is missing
- Unit tests for the expansion function
- cargo fmt/clippy/nextest pass

title: Wire env from provider config to SandboxConfig in orchestrator
description:
The orchestrator currently sets env: Vec::new() at line 380. Update to pass provider-level env vars to SandboxConfig.

Problem: src/orchestrator.rs:380 always sets env to empty Vec.
Solution: Pass env from provider config through to SandboxConfig.

Challenge: Orchestrator is generic over provider type. Options:
1. Add base_env() method to SandboxProvider trait
2. Pass env through Config struct  
3. Store env in Orchestrator struct

Files to modify:
- src/orchestrator.rs
- Possibly src/provider.rs (if adding trait method)

Depends on: code-59 (env field exists in configs)

Acceptance criteria:
- SandboxConfig.env populated from provider config
- LocalProvider continues to work (already has env)
- cargo fmt/clippy/nextest pass

title: Add --env CLI flag to main.rs
description:
Add --env KEY=VALUE flag to the run subcommand to allow runtime environment variable specification.

Files to modify:

Implementation:
- Add `#[arg(long = "env", value_name = "KEY=VALUE")] env_vars: Vec<String>` to Run command
- Parse KEY=VALUE pairs
- Merge with config-defined env vars (CLI takes precedence)
- Pass merged env to orchestrator/provider

Acceptance criteria:
- `offload run --env FOO=bar --env BAZ=qux` works
- CLI env vars override config env vars
- cargo fmt/clippy/nextest pass

title: Pass env to Modal provider call_python_create
description:
Update ModalProvider to pass environment variables to the Python sandbox creation script.

Files to modify:
- src/provider/modal.rs

Changes needed:
1. Update call_python_create() signature to accept env: &[(String, String)]
2. Add --env KEY=VALUE arguments to the command string
3. Update create_sandbox() to pass config.env to call_python_create()

Example command output:
uv run @modal_sandbox.py create im-abc123 --copy-dir=/local:/remote --env=FOO=bar --env=BAZ=qux

Depends on: code-61 (SandboxConfig.env is populated)

Acceptance criteria:
- Env vars are passed as --env arguments to Python script
- Values are properly shell-escaped
- cargo fmt/clippy/nextest pass

title: Add --env option to modal_sandbox.py create command
description:
Update the modal_sandbox.py create command to accept --env options and pass them to Modal sandbox.

Files to modify:
- scripts/modal_sandbox.py

Changes needed:
1. Add `--env` option (multiple=True) to create_from_image command
2. Parse KEY=VALUE format
3. Pass env vars to modal.Sandbox.create() via secrets parameter

Implementation approach:
```python
@click.option('--env', 'env_vars', multiple=True, help='Environment variable (KEY=VALUE)')
def create_from_image(image_id, copy_dirs, env_vars):
    env_dict = {}
    for spec in env_vars:
        if '=' in spec:
            key, value = spec.split('=', 1)
            env_dict[key] = value
    
    secrets = [modal.Secret.from_dict(env_dict)] if env_dict else []
    sandbox = modal.Sandbox.create(app=app, image=image, secrets=secrets, ...)
```

Depends on: code-63 (Rust side passes --env args)

Acceptance criteria:
- --env option accepted and parsed
- Env vars passed to Modal sandbox
- ratchet passes (check STYLE_GUIDE for Python rules)
- Manual test with Modal shows env vars in sandbox

title: Add tests for environment variable expansion
description:
Add unit tests for expand_env_value function using reliably existing/non-existing env vars (no unsafe code).

**Approach**: Use env vars with predictable existence instead of set_var/remove_var (which are unsafe in Rust 2024).

**Test cases:**
1. ${HOME} (always set) → returns non-empty value, no error
2. ${_OFFLOAD_TEST_NONEXISTENT_VAR} (never set) → returns error
3. ${HOME:-fallback} (set) → returns HOME value, not "fallback"
4. ${_OFFLOAD_TEST_MISSING:-fallback} (unset) → returns "fallback"
5. $$ → returns single $
6. Mixed: "prefix_${HOME}_suffix" → contains prefix and suffix
7. ${_OFFLOAD_TEST_MISSING:-} (empty default) → returns empty string

**File:** src/config.rs (add tests module)

**Constraints:**
- NO unsafe code
- NO unwrap()/expect() - tests return Result and use ?
- Tests must be deterministic

title: Fix unsafe and unwrap in config.rs tests
description:
The tests in src/config.rs have unacceptable patterns:

1. Uses unsafe blocks for std::env::set_var/remove_var
2. Uses unwrap() instead of returning Result with ?

Requirements:
- Remove ALL unsafe code
- Remove ALL unwrap() calls - tests must return Result<(), E> and use ?
- If a test cannot be written safely without unsafe env manipulation, DELETE it
- Tests that only test pure functions (no env var manipulation) should be kept

Files to modify:
- src/config.rs

Acceptance criteria:
- No unsafe keyword anywhere in the file
- No unwrap() in tests
- All remaining tests return Result and use ?
- cargo fmt/clippy/nextest pass

title: Fix DefaultProvider to pass env vars to exec commands
description:
The DefaultProvider doesn't pass provider.env variables to remote sandbox execution.

**Problem:**
1. `DefaultProvider::base_env()` at src/provider/default.rs:214-220 correctly returns env vars from config
2. `DefaultSandbox::exec_stream()` at src/provider/default.rs:316-319 builds a shell command but never injects the env vars
3. In contrast, `LocalSandbox::exec_stream()` at src/provider/local.rs:186-191 explicitly sets env vars on the process

**Fix approach:**
Inject env vars into the command string by prepending them as shell exports. In `build_exec_command()`, prefix the command with `KEY1=value1 KEY2=value2 ...` so they're available in the remote sandbox.

The env vars need to be passed from DefaultProvider to DefaultSandbox during `create_sandbox()`.

**Files to modify:**
- src/provider/default.rs

title: Add tests for DefaultSandbox build_exec_command env var propagation
description:
Add unit tests for the build_exec_command method in DefaultSandbox that verify environment variables are properly prefixed to the command.

**Code change being tested (from code-67):**
The build_exec_command method now prepends env vars as KEY=value prefix to commands.

**Test cases needed:**
1. Empty env vars → command has no prefix
2. Single env var → properly prefixed (e.g., 'FOO=bar program args')
3. Multiple env vars → all prefixed in order
4. Env var with special characters (spaces, quotes) → properly shell-quoted
5. Env var with empty value → handled correctly

**Files to modify:**
- src/provider/default.rs (add tests module)

**Implementation notes:**
- Test build_exec_command directly by constructing a DefaultSandbox with known env values
- Verify the returned command string contains the env prefix
- Tests must return Result<(), E> and use ? (no unwrap per STYLE_GUIDE)
- No unsafe code

title: Fix init_config: test_id_format placed at top-level instead of inside [framework]
description: init_config generates TOML with test_id_format as a top-level key, but Config struct expects it inside [framework]. All three framework templates (pytest, cargo, default) are missing test_id_format in the [framework] section. Generated configs fail to deserialize. Fix: move test_id_format into each framework config template string.

title: Resolve duplicate thiserror dependency versions
description:
The codebase has duplicate versions of thiserror (v1.0.69 and v2.0.18).

Details:
- offload directly depends on thiserror v1.0.69
- bollard (docker client) transitively pulls in thiserror v2.0.18
- This creates unnecessary binary bloat

Resolution options:
1. Update bollard dependency to a version that uses thiserror v1.x (if available)
2. Update offload to use thiserror v2.x to match bollard
3. Document as acceptable if no easy resolution exists

Impact: Low priority - builds work fine, but increases binary size slightly.

title: Fix offload-modal.toml: framework fields misplaced in group sections
description: paths, python, extra_args, markers are in [groups.*] but GroupConfig only has retry_count. Move them to [framework]. Also filters should be markers.

title: Add filters field to GroupConfig
description:
Add an optional 'filters' field to GroupConfig in src/config/schema.rs. This field will hold filter strings like '-m not acceptance' or '-k test_name' that are passed to test frameworks during discovery.

Changes needed:
1. Add 'pub filters: Option<String>' field to GroupConfig struct
2. Update doc comments to explain the field
3. Update any test configs that use GroupConfig

Example usage in config:
```toml
[groups.all]
retry_count = 4
filters = "-m not acceptance"
```

title: Update TestFramework::discover to accept filters
description:
Modify the TestFramework trait's discover method to accept an optional filters parameter.

Changes needed:
1. Change signature from 'async fn discover(&self, paths: &[PathBuf])' to 'async fn discover(&self, paths: &[PathBuf], filters: Option<&str>)'
2. Update all implementations (PytestFramework, CargoFramework, DefaultFramework) to accept but ignore the new parameter for now

This is a breaking change to the trait interface that allows filters to be passed during discovery.

title: Update DefaultFramework to substitute {filters} placeholder
description:
Modify DefaultFramework::discover to substitute the {filters} placeholder in discover_command.

Changes needed:
1. In DefaultFramework::discover, substitute '{filters}' in self.config.discover_command with the filters parameter (or empty string if None)
2. The filters are passed as-is (e.g., '-m not acceptance') and get substituted directly

Example:
- discover_command = "uv run pytest --collect-only -q 2>/dev/null '{filters}' | grep '::'"
- filters = "-m not acceptance"
- Result: "uv run pytest --collect-only -q 2>/dev/null '-m not acceptance' | grep '::'"

title: Update PytestFramework to use filters parameter
description:
Modify PytestFramework::discover to use the filters parameter instead of the markers field.

Changes needed:
1. In PytestFramework::discover, if filters is Some, split it and add as args to the pytest command
2. The filters string may contain multiple args like '-m not acceptance -k test_foo'
3. Use shell_words::split to properly parse the filters string into arguments
4. This allows users to pass any pytest flags like -m (markers) or -k (keyword expression)

Note: The existing markers field in PytestFrameworkConfig remains for backward compatibility but filters from the group takes precedence.

title: Update CargoFramework to use filters parameter
description:
Modify CargoFramework::discover to use the filters parameter.

Changes needed:
1. In CargoFramework::discover, if filters is Some, split it and add as args to the cargo nextest list command
2. The filters string may contain nextest filter expressions like '-E test(something)'
3. Use shell_words::split to properly parse the filters string into arguments

Example:
- filters = "-E 'kind(lib)'" would be added to the cargo nextest list command

title: Update main.rs to pass filters from groups during discovery
description:
Modify main.rs to pass the group's filters when calling framework.discover().

Changes needed:
1. In run_tests(), get the filters from the first group config (same pattern as retry_count)
2. Pass filters to the framework's discover method
3. In collect_tests(), also get filters from the first group and pass to discover

Example flow:
```rust
let (group_name, retry_count, filters) = config
    .groups
    .iter()
    .next()
    .map(|(name, cfg)| (name.clone(), cfg.retry_count, cfg.filters.as_deref()))
    .unwrap_or_else(|| ("default".to_string(), 0, None));

// Then pass filters to discover
let tests = PytestFramework::new(cfg.clone()).discover(&[], filters).await?;
```

title: Support multiple groups with different filters
description:
Fix main.rs to properly support multiple groups, each with their own filters.

Current behavior: Only uses the first group from the HashMap, ignoring all others.

Expected behavior:
1. Iterate over ALL groups in config.groups
2. For each group, run discovery with that group's filters
3. Tag discovered tests with the group name and retry_count
4. Combine all tests into a single list for execution

Files to modify:
- src/main.rs: run_tests() and collect_tests() functions

Example config:
```toml
[groups.unit]
retry_count = 2
filters = "-m 'not slow'"

[groups.slow]
retry_count = 2  
filters = "-m 'slow'"
```

Should discover unit tests with '-m not slow' filter, slow tests with '-m slow' filter, and run both sets.

title: Fix CargoFramework to respect nextest filter-match status
description:
The CargoFramework JSON parsing includes ALL tests from nextest list output, ignoring the filter-match status field.

When filters are passed (e.g., -E 'test(/cache/)'), nextest's JSON output marks each test with:
- `"filter-match":{"status":"matches"}` for matching tests
- `"filter-match":{"status":"mismatch","reason":"expression"}` for non-matching tests

Current code in parse_json_output() extracts ALL tests regardless of this status.

Fix needed in src/framework/cargo.rs:
1. Update NextestSuite testcases to deserialize the filter-match info
2. Only include tests where filter-match.status == "matches" (or missing)

title: Make filters field mandatory in GroupConfig
description:
Change the filters field in GroupConfig from Option<String> to String (mandatory).

Changes needed:
1. src/config/schema.rs: Change `pub filters: Option<String>` to `pub filters: String`
2. src/config/schema.rs: Remove `#[serde(default)]` attribute from filters field
3. src/main.rs: Update `filters.as_deref()` calls to `Some(&filters)` or similar
4. src/main.rs: Update init_config to include filters: "" for default group
5. Update all test configs in schema.rs tests to include filters field

Users must now specify filters for every group. Use empty string for no filtering.

title: Rename executor module to orchestrator
description:
Comprehensively rename the 'executor' module to 'orchestrator' since Orchestrator is its key component.

Files to rename:
- src/executor.rs → src/orchestrator.rs
- src/executor/ directory → src/orchestrator/ directory
- src/executor/runner.rs → src/orchestrator/runner.rs
- src/executor/retry.rs → src/orchestrator/retry.rs
- src/executor/scheduler.rs → src/orchestrator/scheduler.rs

References to update:
- lib.rs: pub mod executor → pub mod orchestrator, re-exports
- main.rs: use offload::executor::Orchestrator
- report.rs: use crate::executor::RunResult
- report/junit.rs: use crate::executor::RunResult
- Documentation strings in executor.rs, lib.rs, runner.rs, retry.rs, scheduler.rs

Doc comments referencing offload::executor::* must be updated to offload::orchestrator::*

title: Validate discover_command contains {filters} placeholder
description:
Add validation to ensure the default framework's discover_command contains the {filters} placeholder.

If a user configures a default framework without {filters} in discover_command, it should be a config parse error with a helpful message.

Changes needed:
1. src/config/schema.rs: Add validation in DefaultFrameworkConfig or during config loading
2. The error message should explain that {filters} is required for group filtering to work

Example error:
"Default framework discover_command must contain '{filters}' placeholder for group filtering to work. Got: 'echo test1 test2'"

title: Require at least one group in config
description:
Add validation that config must have at least one group defined. Also remove defensive code that handles empty groups.

Changes needed:
1. src/config.rs: Add validation in validate_config() that config.groups is not empty
2. src/main.rs: Remove the 'groups_to_process' fallback logic in run_tests() that creates a default group when config.groups is empty
3. src/main.rs: Remove the same fallback logic in collect_tests()
4. Simplify the code since we can now assume groups is never empty
close_reason: Completed: Added validation requiring at least one group in config, removed defensive fallback code

title: Bump version from 0.3.4 to 0.4.0 in Cargo.toml, README.md, and SKILL.md
Fix CLI help text: 'generic' should be 'default' for framework option
description: In src/main.rs:88, the CLI help says 'pytest, cargo, generic' but line 630-634 shows the actual values are 'pytest, cargo, default'. The README correctly says 'default'. Fix the help text to say 'default' instead of 'generic'.

title: Add --test and --test-regex flags to offload logs for per-test filtering
Rename project: Update Cargo.toml from offload to offload
description: Update Cargo.toml to rename the package, binary, and library from 'offload' to 'offload'. This is part of the project rename from offload/trawl to offload.

title: Add command and run_args fields to PytestFrameworkConfig in schema.rs with round-trip and backward-compat tests
Use command/run_args in pytest discovery and execution (pytest.rs) with unit tests
Update README.md pytest config table with command and run_args fields
Extract discover_all_tests helper from run_tests
description: Extract lines 235-275 of src/main.rs (test discovery loop) into a standalone async fn discover_all_tests(framework, groups) -> Result<Vec<TestRecord>>. Pure extraction, no behavior change.

title: Extract dispatch_framework helper
description: Add a generic helper fn dispatch_framework<P: SandboxProvider>(config, all_tests, provider, copy_dirs, verbose) -> Result<i32> that matches on framework config and calls run_all_tests. Replaces the 9-arm (provider, framework) match with 3-arm provider match + 3-arm framework dispatch inside the helper.

title: Restructure run_tests for concurrency with tokio::try_join!
description: Restructure run_tests to overlap test discovery with image preparation using tokio::try_join! for Default and Modal providers. Local provider stays sequential since LocalProvider::new() is synchronous. Handle collect_only before provider setup.

title: Reuse discover_all_tests in collect_tests
description: Replace the duplicated discovery loop in collect_tests() (lines 539-570) with a call to discover_all_tests. Small cleanup, separate commit.

title: Add max_batch_size constraint to LPT scheduler
description:
## Directive

IMPORTANT: Before doing ANY git or VCS operations, you MUST activate the jujutsu skill by running /jujutsu. This is a jujutsu-managed repository. Using raw git commands will corrupt data.

When this bead is complete, mark the final revision with a branch: danver/cap-batch-size-lpt

## Problem

The LPT scheduler in src/orchestrator/scheduler.rs produces "mega-batches" of 300+ tests. The `would_fit` method (line 54-63) only checks two constraints: total command string length (`MAX_BATCH_COMMAND_LEN = 30,000`) and an optional duration cap (`max_batch_duration`). There is no constraint on the number of tests per batch.

When hundreds of fast tests each have estimated durations of ~0.01s, the scheduler packs 300+ of them into a single batch because their summed durations stay under the 10-second `MAX_BATCH_DURATION` cap. These mega-batches then take 14-35 seconds in practice (4-10x the estimate), because per-test overhead (fixture setup, import time, etc.) is not captured in historical durations.

## Evidence from Trace Analysis

From Run 3 (26,210 tests, 200 sandboxes, 525 batches):
- Max batch size: 318 tests
- Batches 500-524 each contain 314-318 tests
- These mega-batches take 20-26s actual (vs ~3s estimated)
- The estimation error is the root cause of scheduling imbalance

## Required Changes

### 1. Add `max_batch_size` parameter to `schedule_lpt`

In `src/orchestrator/scheduler.rs`:

Add a new parameter `max_batch_size: Option<usize>` to `schedule_lpt` (line 184). Update the `would_fit` method (line 54-63) to also check:

```rust
fn would_fit(
    &self,
    test_id_len: usize,
    duration: Duration,
    max_batch_duration: Option<Duration>,
    max_batch_size: Option<usize>,
) -> bool {
    self.is_empty()
        || (self.command_len + test_id_len <= MAX_BATCH_COMMAND_LEN
            && max_batch_duration.is_none_or(|cap| self.load + duration <= cap)
            && max_batch_size.is_none_or(|max| self.tests.len() < max))
}
```

### 2. Add `max_batch_size` to configuration

In `src/config/schema.rs`, add `max_batch_size: Option<usize>` to `OffloadConfig` (or whichever struct holds `max_parallel` and `max_batch_duration`). Use `#[serde(default)]` so existing configs work without it.

### 3. Wire it through the orchestrator

In `src/orchestrator.rs`, the call to `scheduler.schedule_lpt` (around line 263) currently passes `Some(MAX_BATCH_DURATION)`. Update this to also pass `max_batch_size` from the config.

### 4. Set a sensible default

When `max_batch_size` is None (not configured), use a default of 50. This is based on trace analysis showing that batches above ~50 tests consistently have >3x estimation error. The default should be a constant like:

```rust
const DEFAULT_MAX_BATCH_SIZE: usize = 50;
```

### 5. Add tests

Add the following test cases to the existing `mod tests` in `src/orchestrator/scheduler.rs`:

- `test_schedule_lpt_respects_max_batch_size`: Create 100 tests with 0.01s durations and max_batch_size=50. Verify no batch exceeds 50 tests.
- `test_schedule_lpt_max_batch_size_creates_extra_batches`: With max_parallel=2 and 200 tests with max_batch_size=50, verify at least 4 batches are created (200/50).
- `test_schedule_lpt_single_test_ignores_batch_size`: A single test that exceeds duration cap still gets its own batch regardless of batch size setting.
- `test_schedule_lpt_batch_size_and_duration_both_enforced`: With both max_batch_duration and max_batch_size set, verify both constraints hold.

### 6. Update existing tests

The existing `schedule_lpt` tests pass `None` for `max_batch_duration` in many cases. Update them to also pass `None` for `max_batch_size` so they continue working.

## Expected Impact

Based on trace analysis, capping at 50 tests per batch should:
- Reduce max batch duration from ~26s to ~8-10s
- Improve tail latency by 10-20s
- Better match estimated vs actual durations

## Files to Modify
- src/orchestrator/scheduler.rs (primary: add max_batch_size to would_fit and schedule_lpt)
- src/config/schema.rs (add max_batch_size config field)
- src/orchestrator.rs (wire config to scheduler call)

title: Remove debug ls /tmp/ exec from download path and reduce I/O overhead
description:
## Directive

IMPORTANT: Before doing ANY git or VCS operations, you MUST activate the jujutsu skill by running /jujutsu. This is a jujutsu-managed repository. Using raw git commands will corrupt data.

When this bead is complete, mark the final revision with a branch: danver/remove-debug-ls-reduce-io

## Problem

The `try_download_results` method in `src/orchestrator/runner.rs` (lines 474-491) executes a debug `ls -la /tmp/` command inside the sandbox before every single result download. This exec is purely diagnostic logging and adds latency to every batch completion. With 525 batches and 200 concurrent sandboxes, these debug execs collectively consume significant I/O time and contribute to contention.

## Evidence from Trace Analysis

From Run 3 (525 batches, 200 sandboxes):
- Mean download time: 8.9s per batch
- Median download time: 7.9s
- Total cumulative download time: 4,685 sandbox-seconds
- Download overhead averages ~22% of batch duration, extending to 50%+ for some batches
- Run 2 (before overlapped discovery) had mean download of 12.8s -- the 200-sandbox concurrent I/O saturates local filesystem

The debug `ls -la /tmp/` is specifically at lines 474-491 of runner.rs:

```rust
// Debug: List /tmp contents before download
info!("[DOWNLOAD] Sandbox {} listing /tmp/ contents...", sandbox_id);
let list_cmd = crate::provider::Command::new("ls").arg("-la").arg("/tmp/");
if let Ok(mut stream) = self.sandbox.exec_stream(&list_cmd).await {
    use futures::StreamExt;
    let mut tmp_contents = Vec::new();
    while let Some(line) = stream.next().await {
        tmp_contents.push(format!("{:?}", line));
    }
    info!("[DOWNLOAD] Sandbox {} /tmp/ contents: {}",
        sandbox_id, tmp_contents.join(" | "));
}
```

This entire block runs for every batch on every sandbox.

## Required Changes

### 1. Remove the debug `ls -la /tmp/` block

In `src/orchestrator/runner.rs`, delete lines 474-491 (the block starting with `// Debug: List /tmp contents before download` through the closing brace of the `if let Ok(mut stream)` block). Keep the actual download logic that follows.

### 2. Guard remaining diagnostic logging behind debug level

The remaining `info!` calls in `try_download_results` (lines 498-501, 504, etc.) are noisy at INFO level with 525 batches. Downgrade the non-error download logging to `debug!` level:
- `[DOWNLOAD] Sandbox {} downloading {}...` -> debug!
- `[DOWNLOAD] Sandbox {} download succeeded` -> debug!
- `[DOWNLOAD] Sandbox {} junit.xml: ... bytes, ... testcases` -> debug!

Keep ERROR-level logs for actual failures (download failed, empty XML, read failed).

### 3. For LocalSandbox: consider skipping download entirely (investigation)

The `LocalSandbox::download()` implementation copies files from the local filesystem. For the local provider, the result XML files at `/tmp/{sandbox_id}.xml` are already on the host filesystem. Investigate whether the download step can be bypassed for the local provider by reading directly from the sandbox temporary directory.

This is an investigation task -- if it is straightforward to implement, do it. If it requires significant refactoring of the download/result-collection interface, document findings and skip the implementation.

### 4. Add tests

If the debug ls removal changes the behavior of `try_download_results` in any observable way (it should not -- it is purely side-effect logging), add a test confirming download still works correctly.

## Expected Impact

- Removing the debug `ls` exec saves ~0.5-2s per batch (exec + stream collection overhead)
- With 525 batches, estimated total savings: 250-1000 sandbox-seconds
- Reducing log noise makes real errors more visible
- Overall wall-clock savings estimated at 5-15s from reduced I/O contention

## Files to Modify
- src/orchestrator/runner.rs (primary: remove debug ls block, downgrade log levels)
- Possibly src/provider/local.rs (if local-provider download bypass is feasible)

title: Make sandbox cleanup async (do not block wall-clock time)
